session_summary: Implemented encounter-based spawn combination system with role-tagged templates
done: Added CombatRole and ThreatClock enums to EnemyEnums.cs
Added design card fields (role, axes, synergies, isDeathSpawnOnly) to EnemyData.cs
Created EncounterTemplate ScriptableObject for role-based group definitions
Created EncounterBuilder static helper to fill template slots from enemy pool with anti-synergy checks
Added encounterTemplates array to WaveConfig (empty array = legacy behavior)
Added encounter spawn path to WaveManager with SpawnEncountersCoroutine, fallback to legacy SpawnLegacyCoroutine
Created SetupEncounterSystem editor script (Tools > Setup Encounter System) to set design card defaults and create 6 starter encounter templates
decisions: Encounter templates use CombatRole slots filled at runtime from pool — not hardcoded enemy references — for flexibility
Anti-synergy check does one re-roll, no complex backtracking, for simplicity
Empty encounterTemplates array falls through to legacy weighted-random path for full backwards compatibility
Inter-group gap is 2x spawnInterval to give visual separation between encounter groups
Created editor script to generate template assets since Unity MCP was unavailable — run Tools > Setup Encounter System after recompile
next_steps: Run Tools > Setup Encounter System in Unity to create the 6 starter template assets and set design card defaults on all EnemyData
Verify recompilation succeeds with no errors
Play-test the arena with encounter templates wired into SurvivalWaveConfig
Set synergyPartners and antiSynergyPartners on EnemyData assets for richer encounter composition
Consider adding more encounter templates for mid/late-game variety
